steps.title: Compare the species' preferences
steps.content: The researchers found that small white herons prefer areas with extensive canopy cover, but glossy ibises do not. A management strategy that increases tree canopy should therefore be expected to appeal more to herons than to glossy ibises.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported outcomes
steps.content: B shifts to lifespan, which the study did not examine. C says canopy increases would decrease appeal to both species, contradicting the heron finding. D introduces non-wetland birds, which are not discussed. The conclusion should compare attraction of glossy ibises with attraction of small white herons. The answer is A.
